title: Action Mandate (delegated authority)
description: The instrument by which a principal grants a software agent bounded, revocable authority to act. A mandate answers the questions at the center of Open-SDE's assured-bounded-autonomy frame: who granted the authority, what it permits, how it is bounded and budgeted, when it expires, and how it is revoked. It implements SDE-0 conformance requirement 2 (a mandate stating scope, budget, duration, target, and revocation conditions) and names the identifiable principal/owner/agent of requirement 1. A mandate is evaluated, never self-executed: a PDP that is independent of the AI decides whether a specific requested action falls within it (schemas/policy-decision.schema.json). Non-claim: authenticating an agent's identity is NOT the same as authorizing a specific action, and holding a token is NOT legal ownership or execution authority. Human involvement is re-scoped, not removed: it moves from per-action approval to setting authority, budgets, monitoring, and revocation, with per-action escalation reserved for the exceptions named in humanInLoopTriggers.

id.description: Stable, globally unique identifier for this mandate. Referenced by policy-decision.mandateRef and execution-receipt.mandateRef.

principal.description: The party on whose behalf and under whose ultimate legal responsibility the agent acts (the grantor of authority).

ownerOperator.description: The party that operates and is accountable for the agent day-to-day. May differ from the principal (for example a managed-service operator acting for a principal customer).

agentId.description: Identifier of the specific software agent this mandate authorizes. This binds authority to one agent identity; identity alone does not confer authorization.

scope.description: The bounded set of actions and resources the agent may touch under this mandate.

allowedActions.description: Enumerated action types permitted (for example 'purchase', 'adjust-setpoint', 'rebalance'). Anything not listed is denied by default.

allowedResources.description: Enumerated resources or targets the actions may be applied to (for example account IDs, device IDs, or resource URNs).

budget.description: The economic ceiling on the mandate. Enforced deterministically at execution time.

currency.description: ISO 4217 code or token/unit symbol the cap and spend are denominated in (for example 'USD').

cap.description: Maximum cumulative amount the agent may commit under this mandate.

spent.description: Cumulative amount already committed. Enforcement MUST deny actions that would push spent above cap.

validity.description: The time window during which the mandate is active (duration bound).

notBefore.description: RFC 3339 instant before which the mandate is not yet effective.

notAfter.description: RFC 3339 instant after which the mandate has expired and must be treated as revoked.

conditions.description: Additional preconditions that must hold for the mandate to apply (for example 'twin-state.confidence >= 0.7' or 'market-open'). Evaluated by the PDP.

obligations.description: Duties the agent or enforcement point must fulfil when acting under this mandate (for example 'log-to-audit', 'notify-owner-on-commit'). Mirrors AuthZEN-style obligations returned with a decision.

humanInLoopTriggers.description: Conditions that require escalation to a human before or during action (for example 'amount > 1000', 'irreversible', 'low-confidence-state'). Implements the shift from out-of-the-loop autonomy to human oversight of exceptions.

revocation.description: Revocation state and policy for the mandate. Revocability is a first-class property of delegated authority.

revocable.description: Whether the mandate can be revoked before natural expiry. Should be true for any real delegated authority.

revokedAt.description: RFC 3339 instant the mandate was revoked, if applicable. Once set, enforcement MUST deny all further actions under this mandate.
